 {"id":"caam-blxo","title":"Implement real-time session token tracking","description":"# Task: Implement real-time session token tracking

## Overview
Track token usage during an active session in real-time, not just from historical logs. This enables accurate burn rate calculation even before logs are written.

## Why This Matters
Historical log parsing is great for:
- Cost analysis
- Long-term trends
- Post-session review

But for LIVE prediction during a session:
- Logs may not be written yet (buffered)
- Want sub-second burn rate updates
- Need to detect usage spikes immediately

## Technical Details

### Session Tracker
```go
// internal/usage/session.go

type SessionTracker struct {
    mu          sync.RWMutex
    entries     []TokenEntry
    windowSize  time.Duration
    
    // Callbacks
    onUsage     func(entry TokenEntry)
}

type TokenEntry struct {
    Timestamp   time.Time
    Model       string
    InputTokens int64
    OutputTokens int64
    Source      string  // "api_response", "log_parse", "stream_estimate"
}

func (t *SessionTracker) Record(entry TokenEntry)
func (t *SessionTracker) BurnRate(window time.Duration) *BurnRateInfo
func (t *SessionTracker) TotalTokens() int64
```

### Integration with SmartRunner
```go
func (r *SmartRunner) handleAPIResponse(resp *Response) {
    // Extract usage from API response headers or body
    if usage := extractUsage(resp); usage != nil {
        r.sessionTracker.Record(TokenEntry{
            Timestamp:    time.Now(),
            Model:        resp.Model,
            InputTokens:  usage.InputTokens,
            OutputTokens: usage.OutputTokens,
            Source:       "api_response",
        })
    }
}
```

### Stream Token Estimation
For streaming responses, estimate tokens in real-time:
```go
func (t *SessionTracker) EstimateFromStream(chunk string) {
    // Rough estimation: ~4 chars per token for English
    estimatedTokens := len(chunk) / 4
    
    t.Record(TokenEntry{
        Timestamp:    time.Now(),
        OutputTokens: int64(estimatedTokens),
        Source:       "stream_estimate",
    })
}
```

### Merging with Log Data
```go
func (t *SessionTracker) MergeLogData(entries []*logs.LogEntry) {
    // Deduplicate based on timestamp/conversation_id
    // Prefer log data over estimates when available
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tracks tokens from API responses in real-time
- [ ] Estimates tokens from streaming output
- [ ] Calculates accurate burn rate from live data
- [ ] Merges with historical log data correctly
- [ ] Thread-safe for concurrent access
- [ ] Memory-efficient (rolling window, not unbounded)

## Test Cases
1. Record and retrieve token entries
2. Calculate burn rate from entries
3. Stream estimation accuracy
4. Merge with log data (deduplication)
5. Thread safety under concurrent writes

## Files to Create
- internal/usage/session.go
- internal/usage/session_test.go

## Dependencies
- Create BurnRateInfo struct (caam-ef8r)

 {"id":"caam-bvd","title":"TUI Backup Feature","description":"# Epic: TUI Backup Feature

## Overview
Add the ability to backup current auth for any coding CLI directly from the TUI, in addition to the existing CLI command.

## Background
Currently, `caam backup <tool> <email>` is CLI-only. Users who prefer the TUI interface must exit to CLI to backup. This creates friction in the workflow.

## User Story
As a user working in the TUI, I want to backup my current auth state without leaving the TUI, so that I can maintain my flow while managing profiles.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Press a key (e.g., 'b') in TUI to initiate backup
- [ ] Text input dialog appears for entering profile name/email
- [ ] Backup executes using existing vault.Backup() logic
- [ ] Success/error feedback displayed in TUI
- [ ] Profile list refreshes automatically after backup
- [ ] Works for all providers (claude, codex, gemini)

## Technical Notes
- TUI uses Bubble Tea framework (internal/tui/)
- Existing backup logic is in internal/authfile/vault.go
- Need to add text input component (charmbracelet/bubbles has textinput)
- Consider: validate email format? Or allow any string?

## Dependencies
None - uses existing infrastructure

## Effort Estimate
Small - primarily TUI wiring and dialog component","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-19T19:37:37.714128841Z","updated_at":"2025-12-19T21:44:34.642714671Z","closed_at":"2025-12-19T21:44:34.642714671Z","close_reason":"Epic complete: Text input dialog (caam-i5i) and TUI backup action (caam-7vg) both closed. All acceptance criteria met: 'b' key triggers backup, dialog prompts for profile name, validation works, backup executes with feedback, profile list refreshes. Tests passing.","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"caam-bxq","title":"Enhanced init wizard with auth discovery","description":"## Overview
Transform the first-run experience from "read docs, run 5 commands" to "run one command, answer prompts, done." The init wizard discovers existing auth files, extracts identities, and guides users through setup.

## The Problem
Current first-time setup requires:
1. Read README to understand concepts
2. Ensure you're logged into each tool
3. Run `caam backup claude <email>` (must know email)
4. Repeat for each tool and account
5. Optionally configure shell integration

This takes 10+ minutes and requires understanding caam's mental model first.

## The Solution
```bash
$ caam init

╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║           Welcome to CAAM - Account Manager               ║
║         Instant switching for AI coding tools             ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Scanning for AI tools and existing sessions...

Found:
  ✓ claude   ~/.claude.json (logged in as alice@gmail.com)
  ✓ codex    ~/.codex/auth.json (logged in as dev@company.com)
  ✗ gemini   not installed

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STEP 1: Save Current Sessions
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

Save your Claude session as a profile?
This lets you switch back to it later.

✓ Saved codex/work

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

STEP 2: Add More Accounts (Optional)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

2. Log in with the other account
3. Come back here

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  claude: personal (active), work-claude

Quick commands:
  caam status        See all profiles
  caam next claude   Switch Claude profile
  caam add codex     Add another Codex account

Happy coding! 🚀
```

## Technical Design

### Auth File Discovery
Scan standard locations for each provider:
```go
func DiscoverAuth(tool string) (*DiscoveredAuth, error) {
    spec := provider.Registry.Get(tool).AuthFiles()
    for _, f := range spec {
        if exists(f.Path) {
            identity, err := extractIdentity(tool, f.Path)
            return &DiscoveredAuth{
                Tool:     tool,
                Path:     f.Path,
                Identity: identity, // email or account ID
                Valid:    err == nil,
            }, nil
        }
    }
    return nil, ErrNoAuth
}
```

### Identity Extraction
Parse auth files to extract user identity:

**Claude** (`~/.claude.json`):
```json
{
  "oauthAccessToken": "...",
  "oauthRefreshToken": "...",
  "claudeAiSessionKey": "sk-ant-...",
  // Look for email in JWT claims or additional fields
}
```
- Decode JWT if present, extract email/sub claim
- Fall back to "unknown" if can't parse

**Codex** (`~/.codex/auth.json`):
```json
{
  "access_token": "...",
  "user": {
    "email": "dev@company.com",
    "name": "Developer"
  }
}
```
- Direct field access

**Gemini** (`~/.gemini/settings.json`):
```json
{
  "account": {
    "email": "user@gmail.com"
  }
}
```
- Direct field access

### Wizard Flow Controller
```go
type WizardState struct {
    Discovered   []DiscoveredAuth
    SavedProfiles []string
    AddedAccounts []string
    ShellSetup   bool
}

func RunWizard() error {
    state := &WizardState{}
    
    // Phase 1: Discovery
    state.Discovered = discoverAllAuth()
    showDiscoveryResults(state.Discovered)
    
    // Phase 2: Save existing
    for _, auth := range state.Discovered {
        if promptSave(auth) {
            name := promptProfileName(auth.Identity)
            vault.Backup(auth.Tool, name)
            state.SavedProfiles = append(state.SavedProfiles, name)
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 3: Add more (optional loop)
    for {
        if !promptAddMore() {
            break
        }
        runAddAccountFlow()
    }
    
    // Phase 4: Shell integration
    if promptShellIntegration() {
        installShellInit()
        state.ShellSetup = true
    }
    
    // Phase 5: Summary
    showSummary(state)
    return nil
}
```

### Interactive Prompts
Using bubbletea or simple stdin for prompts:
- Yes/No prompts with smart defaults
- Text input with default values
- Progress indicators during operations
- Clear section headers

## Configuration Created
After wizard completes:
```yaml
# ~/.caam/config.yaml
version: 1
defaults:
  claude: personal
  codex: work
shell:
  auto_aliases: true
  completion: true
```

## Edge Cases

### No auth files found
```
No AI tool sessions found.

Would you like to:
  [1] Log in to Claude now
  [2] Log in to Codex now  
  [3] Log in to Gemini now
  [4] Exit and set up later

Choice [4]: 
```

### Can't parse identity
```
Found Claude auth at ~/.claude.json
Could not determine account email.
Profile name: _____  (no default suggested)
```

### Existing vault profiles
```
Found existing CAAM profiles:
  claude: work, personal
  codex: main

Merge with new setup or start fresh?
  [M]erge - keep existing, add new
  [F]resh - backup existing, start over
  [C]ancel

Choice [M]:
```

### Multiple accounts already
```
You have 3 Claude accounts saved.
Would you like to add more? [y/N]:
```

## Flags
```bash
caam init              # Interactive wizard
caam init --quick      # Auto-save all discovered, skip prompts
caam init --shell=zsh  # Pre-specify shell type
caam init --no-shell   # Skip shell integration step
caam init --reset      # Clear existing and start fresh
```

## Implementation Tasks
1. **Auth discovery** (internal/discovery/scanner.go)
   - Scan auth file locations
   - Check file existence and readability

2. **Identity extraction** (internal/discovery/identity.go)
   - Parse each provider's auth format
   - JWT decoding for tokens
   - Graceful fallback on parse failure

3. **Wizard UI** (cmd/caam/cmd/init.go)
   - Section-based flow
   - Interactive prompts
   - Progress feedback

4. **Shell integration installer** (cmd/caam/cmd/shell_init.go)
   - Detect shell type
   - Append to rc file
   - Idempotent (don't duplicate)

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Discovers auth for all three providers
- [ ] Extracts identity (email) when possible
- [ ] Saves discovered sessions as profiles
- [ ] Guides through adding additional accounts
- [ ] Installs shell integration optionally
- [ ] Works with no existing auth (guides to first login)
- [ ] Handles existing profiles gracefully
- [ ] Completes in <2 minutes for typical user

## Dependencies
- Vault.Backup() for saving profiles
- Provider auth file specs
- JWT parsing for identity extraction
- Shell init infrastructure (caam-k9z)

## Priority: P0 (Critical for Adoption)
First impressions matter.  {"id":"caam-by4","title":"Terminal UI (TUI) with Bubble Tea","description":"## Overview
A visual terminal interface for managing caam profiles. While the CLI is fully functional, a TUI provides:
- At-a-glance view of all profiles across providers
- Visual feedback on status (logged in, locked, active)
- Discoverable interface for new users
- Running session management

## Design (from original spec)
Layout:
- Left panel: Provider list (Codex / Claude / Gemini)
- Center panel: Profiles table (Name, Auth mode, Logged-in?, Last used, Notes)
- Right panel: Action pane + detail view for selected profile

Keybindings:
- ↑/↓: Select profile
- Enter: Run default command (launch CLI)
- l: Login/refresh auth
- o: Open account page in browser
- e: Edit profile (notes, browser config)
- d: Delete profile (with confirmation)
- /: Search/filter profiles
- Tab: Switch between providers
- q: Quit

## Implementation
Use Bubble Tea (bubbletea) with:
- bubbles for standard components (list, table, textinput)
- lipgloss for styling

## User Value
- Visual overview prevents mistakes (wrong account)
- Running sessions visible prevents data corruption
- Lower learning curve than CLI-only

## Consideration
TUI should be the default when running 'caam' with no args, but all functionality must remain available via CLI for scripting.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T05:51:16.807075968Z","updated_at":"2025-12-17T06:46:22.843793337Z","closed_at":"2025-12-17T06:46:22.843793337Z","close_reason":"All TUI components implemented: scaffolding (caam-qpx), provider panel (caam-ta7), profiles table (caam-nmm), detail panel (caam-f9h), keyboard navigation (caam-j14). TUI is fully functional with three-panel layout and all keybindings wired up.","compaction_level":0}

 {"id":"caam-byup","title":"Implement monitor output renderers (table/brief/JSON)","description":"# Task: Implement monitor output renderers (table/brief/JSON)

## Overview
Create multiple output renderers for the monitor state, supporting different use cases from rich TUI displays to tmux status bar integration.

## Renderers

### Table Renderer (default)
Rich ASCII table for interactive terminal use:
```
╔═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                    LIVE USAGE MONITOR                                  ║
║                    Last updated: 12:34:56                              ║
╠═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╣
║  CLAUDE                                                                 ║
║  alice@gmail.com  🟢 ████████░░░░░░░░░░░░ 42% │ resets 4h 12m │ ready  ║
║  bob@gmail.com    🟡 ██████████████░░░░░░ 71% │ resets 1h 45m │ ready  ║
╚═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝
```

### Brief Renderer
One-line summary for tmux/status bar:
```
claude:42%▲ codex:18%▲
```

### JSON Renderer
Machine-readable for scripting:
```json
{
  "updated_at": "2025-01-10T12:34:56Z",
  "profiles": {...}
}
```

### Alert Renderer
Alerts-only mode for logging:
```
[2025-01-10 12:34:56] ⚠️  claude/carol@gmail.com at 85%
[2025-01-10 12:35:12] 🔴 claude/carol@gmail.com at 95% - SWITCH RECOMMENDED
```

## Technical Details

### Renderer Interface
```go
type Renderer interface {
    Render(state *MonitorState) string
}

type TableRenderer struct {
    width int
    showEmoji bool
}

type BriefRenderer struct {
    separator string
}

type JSONRenderer struct {
    pretty bool
}

type AlertRenderer struct {
    threshold float64
    history   []*Alert
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Table renderer produces clean ASCII output
- [ ] Brief renderer fits in tmux status bar (<80 chars)
- [ ] JSON renderer produces valid, parseable JSON
- [ ] Alert renderer tracks alert history (no duplicates)
- [ ] All renderers handle edge cases (no profiles, all errors, etc.)

## Files to Create
- internal/monitor/render.go
- internal/monitor/render_table.go
- internal/monitor/render_brief.go
- internal/monitor/render_json.go
- internal/monitor/render_alert.go
- internal/monitor/render_test.go

 {"id":"caam-ldjp","title":"Create enhanced test harness with detailed logging","description":"# Create Enhanced Test Harness

## Goal
Extend testutil.Harness with comprehensive structured logging for all e2e tests.

## Current Harness Location
internal/testutil/harness.go

## Required Enhancements

### Structured Logging
```go
type ExtendedHarness struct {
    *Harness
    logBuffer   *bytes.Buffer
    stepLogs    []StepLog
    metrics     map[string]time.Duration
}

type StepLog struct {
    Name      string
    Timestamp time.Time
    Duration  time.Duration
    Level     LogLevel
    Message   string
    Data      map[string]interface{}
}
```

### Required Methods
```go
func (h *ExtendedHarness) StartStep(name, description string)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) EndStep(name string)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) LogInfo(msg string, data ...interface{})
func (h *ExtendedHarness) LogDebug(msg string, data ...interface{})
func (h *ExtendedHarness) LogError(err error, context string)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) RecordMetric(name string, value time.Duration)
func (h *ExtendedHarness) DumpLogs() string
func (h *ExtendedHarness) Summary() string
```

### Features
1. Timestamp each log entry
2. Track duration of each step
3. JSON-formatted structured data
4. Automatic cleanup logging
5. Test summary with metrics

## File: internal/testutil/extended_harness.go

- Clear, readable log output
- Supports nested steps
- Metrics collection for performance tests","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-20T00:06:06.776731881Z","updated_at":"2025-12-20T01:34:11.179742332Z","closed_at":"2025-12-20T01:34:11.179742332Z","close_reason":"Implemented ExtendedHarness with step tracking, logging, metrics, and summary methods.  {"id":"caam-ldxo","title":"Epic: Installation, Updates, and Dependency Bootstrap","description":"Purpose
- Make caam installs, updates, and dependency management bulletproof, idempotent, and user-friendly across macOS/Linux, including multi-machine setups.

Background
- Current install.sh fetches GitHub releases but does not verify checksums or signatures, has limited cache-busting, and lacks a built-in self-update command.
- Local repos (repo_updater, xf) already demonstrate stronger patterns: checksum verification, cache-busting, optional gum UI, and interactive confirmations.
- Recent tooling ecosystem trends emphasize signed release artifacts and explicit verification (e.g., cosign-verified checksums in Charm tools).

Scope
- Harden install.sh with checksum/signature verification, cache-busting, and safe fallbacks.
- Add caam update/self-update workflows with atomic replace and rollback.
- Provide dependency bootstrap (gum, wezterm, tailscale, playwright, gh, jq) with safe prompts and non-interactive mode.
- Support auto-update scheduling (opt-in) and distributed update orchestration.
- Add comprehensive installer/update tests and logging.

Non-Goals
- Shipping a package manager; use system package managers when possible.

Success Criteria
- Installs verify integrity, upgrades are idempotent, and missing dependencies are handled gracefully.
- Updates and dependency bootstrap are auditable, reversible, and do not surprise users.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:00.356724426Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:00.356724426Z","compaction_level":0}
-{"id":"caam-ldxo.1","title":"Task: Release artifacts signing + checksum verification","description":"Goal
- Ensure every release includes machine-verifiable integrity (checksums + signatures) and the installer consumes those artifacts.

Background
- caam install.sh currently does not verify checksums or signatures.
- Charm tools now ship signed checksums (cosign), which is a good integrity baseline.

Details
- Update release pipeline (goreleaser) to publish SHA256SUMS + SHA256SUMS.sig (cosign).
- Decide on key distribution (cosign public key embedded in repo OR keyless GitHub OIDC with Rekor log verification).
- Include an explicit release manifest (version, build date, git SHA, go version) for verification + debugging.
- Document verification commands for users (cosign verify-blob + sha256sum).
- Keep signing optional for local snapshots, required for tagged releases.

Tests
- CI job that fails if release artifacts lack checksum + signature.
- Test harness that validates checksum files against a staged artifact and verifies signature.

Success
- Every tagged release includes checksums and signatures, and installer/update flows verify them before install.

+{"id":"caam-ldxo.1","title":"Task: Release artifacts signing + checksum verification","description":"Goal
- Ensure every release includes machine-verifiable integrity (checksums + signatures) and the installer consumes those artifacts.

Background
- caam install.sh currently does not verify checksums or signatures.
- Charm tools now ship signed checksums (cosign), which is a good integrity baseline.

Details
- Update release pipeline (goreleaser) to publish SHA256SUMS + SHA256SUMS.sig (cosign).
- Decide on key distribution (cosign public key embedded in repo OR keyless GitHub OIDC with Rekor log verification).
- Include an explicit release manifest (version, build date, git SHA, go version) for verification + debugging.
- Document verification commands for users (cosign verify-blob + sha256sum).
- Keep signing optional for local snapshots, required for tagged releases.

Tests
- CI job that fails if release artifacts lack checksum + signature.
- Test harness that validates checksum files against a staged artifact and verifies signature.

Success
- Every tagged release includes checksums and signatures, and installer/update flows verify them before install.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:07.650248513Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:20.776220565Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:31:20.776138841Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.1","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:07.651430109Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.2","title":"Task: Harden install.sh (integrity + gum UX + cache-busting)","description":"Goal
- Make the installer safer, clearer, and more reliable while remaining a copy/paste one-liner.

Background
- Local installers (repo_updater, xf) show best practices: cache-busting, gum-enhanced output, non-interactive mode, and checksum verification.
- We need parity for caam with explicit integrity checks and predictable logging.

Details
- Add checksum verification + signature validation (from caam-ldxo.1).
- Add cache-busting query param in URLs (opt-out env var).
- Add gum-based formatting if gum is installed and stdout is a TTY; graceful fallback otherwise.
- Add NO_COLOR + CI/non-interactive behavior (no prompts, deterministic exit codes).
- Add explicit --verify option (run caam --version and optional self-test).
- Add explicit --channel=stable|beta and --version override.
- Ensure install is idempotent: if the same version is installed, skip download unless --force.
- Preserve previous binary as backup and restore if replace fails.
- Improve error messages with actionable guidance (missing curl/wget, PATH, permissions).

Tests
- Shell tests for: checksum failure, signature failure, gum present/absent, non-tty, cache-bust on/off, version already installed, rollback on partial failure.
- Mock GitHub API responses to validate parsing and fallback behavior.

Success
- Installer is safe, audit-friendly, and pleasant to read in the terminal.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:18.723957303Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:53:09.289837881Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.2","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:18.725403277Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.2","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:19.701293431Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.3","title":"Task: Self-update command (caam update)","description":"Goal
- Provide an in-product, idempotent update path that verifies integrity and supports rollback.

Details
- Add `caam update` (and `caam update --check`) to fetch latest release metadata.
- Verify SHA256 + signature before replacing binary.
- Use atomic replace with temp file + rename; keep last-known-good binary for rollback.
- Support channels (stable/beta) and explicit version pinning.
- Emit structured logs (version_from, version_to, download_url, checksum_ok, signature_ok).
- Honor non-interactive mode (CI) with clear exit codes for "up-to-date", "updated", "failed".

Tests
- Unit tests for release metadata parsing and version comparison.
- Integration test with a fake local release server and bad checksum/signature cases.
- E2E test that verifies rollback restores previous version on failure.

Success
- Users can upgrade safely without re-running curl | bash.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:25.027082534Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T21:53:15.563573020Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:50:25.028567021Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-ldxo.3","depends_on_id":"caam-ldxo.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T18:51:31.725586041Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-ldxo.4","title":"Task: Auto-update scheduler + distributed fleet upgrade","description":"Goal
- Offer opt-in background update checks and coordinated upgrades across remote coordinators.

Details
- Add a scheduler (daemon/cron helper) to run `caam update --check` daily and surface notifications in TUI/CLI.
- For distributed auth recovery, provide `caam update --remotes` to update coordinator binaries via SSH/SFTP with checksum verification.
- Maintain idempotency: skip if versions match, and provide a --dry-run report.
- Add a rollback command to revert remote nodes to last-known-good.
- Ensure update checks are opt-in by default and never auto-apply without explicit consent.

Tests
- Dry-run snapshot tests for local + remote update plans.
- Mock SSH/SFTP tests to validate version detection, upgrade ordering, and rollback.
- Log validation tests to ensure structured logs include node, version, and outcome.

Success
- Users can keep single or multi-machine setups current with minimal friction.

@@ -43,17 +43,67 @@ jobs:
           tag="${GITHUB_REF#refs/tags/}"
           api="https://api.github.com/repos/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/releases/tags/${tag}"
           release_json=$(curl -fsSL -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GITHUB_TOKEN}" -H "Accept: application/vnd.github+json" "$api")
-          RELEASE_JSON="$release_json" python - <<-PY
+          parsed=$(RELEASE_JSON="$release_json" python - <<-PY
           	import json
           	import os
           	import sys
 
           	data = json.loads(os.environ.get("RELEASE_JSON", "{}"))
-          	names = {asset.get("name") for asset in data.get("assets", [])}
-          	required = {"SHA256SUMS", "SHA256SUMS.sig"}
-          	missing = sorted(required - names)
-          	if missing:
-          	    sys.stderr.write("Missing release assets: " + ", ".join(missing) + "\n")
+          	assets = data.get("assets", [])
+
+          	def find(name: str) -> str:
+          	    for asset in assets:
+          	        if asset.get("name") == name:
+          	            return asset.get("browser_download_url") or ""
+          	    return ""
+
+          	checksums_url = find("SHA256SUMS")
+          	signature_url = find("SHA256SUMS.sig")
+
+          	sample = None
+          	for asset in assets:
+          	    name = asset.get("name") or ""
+          	    if name in {"SHA256SUMS", "SHA256SUMS.sig"}:
+          	        continue
+          	    if name.endswith(".tar.gz") or name.endswith(".zip"):
+          	        sample = asset
+          	        break
+
+          	if not checksums_url or not signature_url:
+          	    sys.stderr.write("Missing SHA256SUMS or SHA256SUMS.sig assets\n")
+          	    sys.exit(1)
+          	if not sample:
+          	    sys.stderr.write("Missing archive asset to verify\n")
           	    sys.exit(1)
-          	print("Release assets verified:", ", ".join(sorted(required)))
+
+          	print(checksums_url)
+          	print(signature_url)
+          	print(sample.get("name") or "")
+          	print(sample.get("browser_download_url") or "")
           	PY
+          )
+
+          checksums_url=$(printf '%s' "$parsed" | sed -n '1p')
+          signature_url=$(printf '%s' "$parsed" | sed -n '2p')
+          sample_name=$(printf '%s' "$parsed" | sed -n '3p')
+          sample_url=$(printf '%s' "$parsed" | sed -n '4p')
+
+          tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"
+          curl -fsSL "$checksums_url" -o "$tmpdir/SHA256SUMS"
+          curl -fsSL "$signature_url" -o "$tmpdir/SHA256SUMS.sig"
+          curl -fsSL "$sample_url" -o "$tmpdir/$sample_name"
+
+          cosign verify-blob \
+            --bundle "$tmpdir/SHA256SUMS.sig" \
+            --certificate-oidc-issuer "https://token.actions.githubusercontent.com" \
+            --certificate-identity "https://github.com/${GITHUB_REPOSITORY}/.github/workflows/release.yml@refs/tags/${tag}" \
+            "$tmpdir/SHA256SUMS"
+
+          expected=$(grep -F " $sample_name" "$tmpdir/SHA256SUMS" | awk '{print $1}' | head -1)
+          actual=$(sha256sum "$tmpdir/$sample_name" | awk '{print $1}')
+          if [ -z "$expected" ] || [ "$expected" != "$actual" ]; then
+            echo "Checksum mismatch for $sample_name" >&2
+            exit 1
+          fi
+
+          echo "Release assets verified: SHA256SUMS + signature + archive checksum"
